name: Ron Coleman Mining
short_description: Fee dig operation in the Garland County quartz mining region. Search for quartz crystals, feldspar, and mica in a working quarry setting near Hot Springs.
description:
Ron Coleman Mining is a fee-based rockhounding venue located in Garland County, Arkansas, near Hot Springs. This area is part of the historic Ouachita Mountain mining district, where quartz and feldspar have been commercially mined for over a century. Visitors can work in designated areas to collect quartz specimens, feldspar crystals, and mica, which are abundant in the local pegmatite and granitic deposits. The site operates seasonally and welcomes rockhounders of all experience levels. Arkansas quartz is prized by collectors for its clarity and crystal formation. The Ouachita region remains one of the premier domestic sources for quality quartz specimens and gemstone materials.

The terrain here is relatively flat at 865 feet elevation, making it accessible for families and casual collectors. The operation typically provides equipment access or allows hand tools for surface collection. This is an ideal location for beginners learning to identify and extract quartz and feldspar specimens. The surrounding Garland County area has a strong rockhounding heritage, with multiple public and private collection sites nearby.
history: The Ouachita Mountains in this region were formed during the Ouachita Orogeny, a major mountain-building event during the Paleozoic Era that created the granitic and metamorphic rocks hosting quartz and feldspar veins. Commercial quartz mining in Garland County dates back to the late 1800s, with these pegmatitic deposits prized for both gemstone and industrial applications.
tips: Focus your search in open pit areas where fresh material is exposed - quartz crystals often cluster in pegmatite pockets. Bring a small pick or chisel and heavy hammer to carefully extract specimens from the surrounding host rock without damaging the crystals. Look for glassy, transparent to milky quartz with visible terminations, and check for feldspar showing pink or white coloration alongside the quartz.
terrain_notes: Flat to gently rolling terrain at 865 feet elevation. The site features an active quarry setting with worked areas and exposed rock faces. Footing is generally firm but uneven due to mining operations. Most collection areas are within a short walk from parking. Bring sturdy boots for protection against sharp edges and loose rock fragments.
best_season: April - October. Spring and early fall offer comfortable temperatures for outdoor work. Summer heat can be intense; start early in the day. Winter months may have limited operation hours or access.
written_directions: From Hot Springs, take US 270 west toward Langley. The site is located in the Garland County quartz mining district, approximately 8-10 miles west of downtown Hot Springs. Look for operational mining company signage and parking areas marking the entrance. Contact Ron Coleman Mining directly for current access directions and operating hours.
road_conditions: Paved highway access with gravel/dirt parking and internal roads. High-clearance vehicles recommended for rough internal roads.
parking_notes: On-site parking available near the quarry operation area. Capacity varies seasonally. Ask staff upon arrival.
collection_rules: This is a private fee dig operation. Visitors must pay an entry or collection fee. Collection is limited to designated areas specified by the operator. Keep all specimens you collect during your visit. No commercial resale of collected material. Follow all operator safety rules and stay within marked boundaries.
quantity_limits: Keep what you find within your paid dig area and time limit.
rules:
Pay required entry or collection fee
Collect only in designated areas
Follow all operator safety guidelines
No commercial use or resale
Stay within marked boundaries
Return tools if provided
nearest_services: Hot Springs, approximately 8-10 miles east. Full services including gas, food, lodging, and supplies available.
accessibility_notes: Relatively flat terrain accessible to most visitors. Uneven ground and loose rock from mining operations require sturdy footwear. No wheelchair access in active collection areas. Physical effort needed to extract specimens with hand tools.

faq.question: What gemstones can you find at Ron Coleman Mining in Arkansas?
faq.answer: Ron Coleman Mining is known for quartz crystals, feldspar, and mica. The site is located in the Garland County quartz mining region, which has produced high-quality quartz specimens for over a century. Visitors commonly find clear to milky quartz with visible crystal terminations, pink or white feldspar, and mica sheets in the pegmatitic deposits.

faq.question: How much does it cost to rockhound at Ron Coleman Mining near Hot Springs?
faq.answer: Ron Coleman Mining operates as a fee dig site. Specific fees vary and may include entry charges or collection-based pricing. We recommend contacting the operator directly to confirm current fees, group rates, and whether equipment rental or tool use is included in your visit.

faq.question: Is Ron Coleman Mining beginner-friendly for first-time rockhounders?
faq.answer: Yes, Ron Coleman Mining is an excellent choice for beginners. The flat 865-foot elevation terrain is accessible to most visitors, and hand tools are adequate for collecting quartz and feldspar specimens. The operator can provide guidance on where to search and how to safely extract crystals from the host rock.

faq.question: What tools do I need to bring to Ron Coleman Mining to collect quartz?
faq.answer: A small pick or chisel and a heavy hammer are essential for safely extracting quartz specimens from the pegmatitic host rock. Wear sturdy boots for protection against sharp edges and loose rock. Bring a collection bag and safety glasses. Contact the site ahead of time to see if they provide any tools or if rentals are available.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit Ron Coleman Mining for rockhounding in Arkansas?
faq.answer: April through October offers the best conditions, with spring and early fall providing comfortable temperatures. Summer visits are possible but start early in the day to avoid intense heat. Winter months may have limited operation hours. Call ahead to confirm seasonal hours before planning your trip.

faq.question: How far is Ron Coleman Mining from Hot Springs, and what services are nearby?
faq.answer: Ron Coleman Mining is located approximately 9 miles west of Hot Springs in Garland County. Hot Springs offers full amenities including gas stations, restaurants, hotels, and supply shops. The site's proximity to town makes it convenient for a day trip while still providing authentic Arkansas quartz mining country.

meta_description: Fee dig site for quartz, feldspar, and mica near Hot Springs, Arkansas. Beginner-friendly collection in the Garland County quartz mining region.
